profile.name: AdityaSinha
profile.bio: Just a New developer doing things to become Great.

prs.title: feat: Hindi UI translation & language switching fix
prs.body:
## Changes
- Replaced fragile Google Translate with a robust localStorage-based language system
- Added a comprehensive Hindi translation dictionary (30+ UI elements)
- Auto-translates nav items, page titles, categories, labels, placeholders on language switch
- Language preference persists across page reloads via localStorage
- Works reliably on both localhost and Render deployments

prs.title: feat: MP3 meditation audio, YouTube timer, bilingual media switching
prs.body:
## Changes
- Replaced YouTube videos on Meditation page with pure MP3 audio for a cleaner experience
- Implemented YouTube IFrame API on Calm Videos page with automatic session timer (pauses video at the specified duration)
- Added bilingual (English/Hindi) media switching for videos
- Fixed Deep Focus content to use nature/ambient sounds
- Added progress bar and session completion notifications
- Cache-busting for script tags to ensure latest content loads

prs.title: Refactor Wellness Features: Videos, Meditations, and Auth System
prs.body: This PR implements real video/audio content via YouTube embeds, fixes Hindi translation logic, and adds a premium authentication modal.

prs.title: feat: Mixpanel integration & Hex.tech seed data
prs.body: This PR adds Mixpanel analytics integration and a seed data script for Hex.tech.

prs.title: grammar error
prs.body:
This PR corrects several typos, grammar errors, and outdated references in the
Mongoose documentation. These fixes improve clarity and accuracy across both
`README.md` and `CONTRIBUTING.md`.

prs.title: fix(theme): add fallback to colors.foreground when colors.editor.foreground is missing

labels.name: seems-ai-generated
labels.description: We label this as a rejection to PR that seems to be AI generated. Please do not do that.

prs.body:
This PR fixes an issue where themes that define `colors.foreground` (but not
`colors.editor.foreground`) incorrectly fell back to the default foreground color
(`#bbbbbb`) during theme normalization.  

With this change, the Aurora-X theme and similar themes now correctly expose their
intended foreground color.

fixes: #978

prs.title: i18n: Update Punjabi (Pakistan) translations and add missing keys
prs.body:
This PR updates the Punjabi (Pakistan) localization file (`src/i18n/locales/pa-pk.js`) by
adding all missing translation keys to ensure full parity with the English locale
(`en.js`). The file now includes every key required by the application’s current i18n
structure.

prs.title: feat: fix enum array cast syntax in PostgreSQL adapter
prs.body:
This PR implements a workaround in the adapter-pg TypeScript runtime to correct incorrectly generated enum array cast syntax produced by the Rust query compiler (prisma-engines). The compiler currently produces SQL like:

cast($1 as "public"."Role[]")

which is invalid. The correct SQL is:

cast($1 as "public"."Role"[])

Because the Rust code that emits the SQL requires a separate change (and to avoid breaking the engines at once), this PR post-processes SQL strings in the runtime and fixes the specific incorrect pattern before executing the query.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Fixed SQL cast syntax for enum array types in PostgreSQL queries. The adapter now correctly transforms enum array cast patterns to ensure proper query execution.

* **Tests**
  * Added functional test coverage for enum array field updates across PostgreSQL and CockroachDB database providers.

<sub>✏️ Tip: You can customize this high-level summary in your review settings.</sub>

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

issues.title: Light theme code blocks render with forced white background
issues.body:
### Validations

- [x] Follow our [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/shikijs/shiki/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
- [x] Read the [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/shikijs/shiki/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).
- [x] Check that there isn't already an issue that reports the same bug to avoid creating a duplicate.

### Describe the bug

When using Shiki with light themes (e.g., github-light, min-light, others), the generated HTML always includes:

style="background-color:#fff;color:#24292e"


This results in all highlighted code blocks having a forced white background, even when the intention is to let the site/theme CSS control the background (e.g., transparent backgrounds).

This behavior is incorrect and breaks theming in frameworks such as VitePress, Astro, Next.js, etc.

### Reproduction

import { getHighlighter } from 'shiki'  const highlighter = await getHighlighter({   theme: 'github-light' })  console.log(await highlighter.codeToHtml('const x = 1'))

### Contributes

- [x] I am willing to submit a PR to fix this issue
- [x] I am willing to submit a PR with failing tests

issues.title: Copy-to-Clipboard Button Not Showing on Code Blocks
issues.body: When building the documentation, the copy-to-clipboard button does not appear on individual code blocks. This makes it difficult for users to quickly copy code snippets.

issues.title: Missing “Copy” Button for Code Blocks in Post-Processing Documentation
issues.body:
### Description

Location:
Three.js Manual → Post Processing
(e.g., /docs/#manual/en/introduction/Post-processing)

Issue:
The code examples in this page do not provide a "Copy" button, which makes it less convenient for users to copy example code directly for testing. Many modern documentation pages include an inline "Copy to Clipboard" button for code blocks.

Why this matters:
Post-processing examples are long and typed frequently by users.
New learners especially benefit from quick-copy code blocks.
This improves documentation usability.

### Solution

Add a small UI button to all <pre><code> blocks in the documentation pages that copies the block’s text to the clipboard.

### Alternatives

Ctrl + A, then Ctrl + C (Windows/Linux)

Cmd + A, then Cmd + C (Mac)

This selects the whole block even if no "Copy" button exists.

### Additional context

If maintainers agree, I can also submit a PR to add this functionality to the documentation template.
